name: Avatar Hook + Beat-Locked B-Roll Short
description: 9:16 short that opens on a Seedance avatar selfie hook (lip-synced via ElevenLabs reference audio, native clip audio KEPT for the hook), then cuts to a fast-paced body (~2s cuts) of beat-locked b-roll — real screen recordings, documentary motion clips, and Ken Burns stills — with single-word serif karaoke captions, a topic badge during the hook, and a 'comment X and I'll send you Y' CTA card. Born from Borja's 'Claude Mythos SEO Test' template (2026-06-10).
best_for: trending-topic shorts for a niche audience (trend + niche angle)
DFY YouTube Shorts / Instagram Reels for Distribb customers
comment-gated lead magnet CTAs (comment X, get Y)
product surfaces or niche b-roll narrated by a consistent UGC character
not_for: videos where the avatar must speak past the hook

stages.title: Script + voice-first VO
actions: Write original script: hook (<=8s, 1-2 SHORT sentences), 3-4 body beats, spoken 'comment X and I'll send you Y' CTA
Pick a gender-matched ElevenLabs voice for the character; generate full VO with word timestamps (elevenlabs_tts_with_timestamps)
VERIFY VO gender by pitch (median <165 Hz male, >165 Hz female) before any paid video call
Slice the hook words into hook_vo.wav using word timestamps

stages.title: Seedance avatar hook clip
actions: Lock a character card + reference image (user-provided or text-only gpt-image-2; NEVER derived from the user's own footage)
Generate ONE 6s 9:16 reference-to-video clip: --reference-image <character> --reference-audio hook_vo.wav --generate-audio, prompt includes the exact hook line and any pattern interrupt
KEEP the clip's native audio for the hook (reference audio clones the VOICE but NOT timing; mute-and-overlay drifts >1s)
QC: Whisper the clip (exact words), pitch-check the cloned voice gender, frame-check character match

stages.title: Beat-locked b-roll harvest/generation
actions: Map each narration line to a visual using vo_words.json timings
Prefer real surfaces: logged-in product screen recordings (Playwright; record_video_size MUST equal viewport) or the customer's live published pages/images
If real assets are low-res or missing: generate documentary-realism Seedance text-to-video motion clips (no audio) + gpt-image-2 stills; cut every ~2-3s, never hold one still >3.5s
NO slow text scrolls — text-heavy blog scrolling kills retention (Borja feedback 2026-06-10)

stages.title: Timeline assembly
actions: Video: hook clip (6s) -> ~12 fast cuts beat-locked to VO -> CTA card with push-in; CTA duration = audio_dur - body_sum so -shortest never clips the spoken CTA
Audio: hook native 0-6s, acrossfade (0.12s) into vo_master from just before the first body word, loudnorm I=-16:TP=-1.5:LRA=11
Overlays: Pillow topic badge top-left during hook (0-2.4s); single-word Times New Roman karaoke captions (ASS Alignment=2, MarginV~240, Outline 5), clamp word end-times to next word start
FFmpeg gotchas: zoompan on stills = single image input + -frames:v N (never -loop 1 -t); zoompan on video = d=1 + trim

stages.title: QC and delivery
actions: Run ffmpeg_qc (1080x1920 h264/aac yuv420p, no black frames)
Frame-sheet review: captions legible on light b-roll, no overlay collisions, every cut matches its narration line
Listen check: hook-native -> VO voice seam at the 6s cut
Copy the final MP4 to ~/Desktop (the skill tmp dir is hidden from Finder)

script_shape: Hook (<=8s, avatar): trend + why care, 1-2 short sentences
Body beat 1-4 (b-roll): concrete specifics, each beat = one visual
Spoken CTA: 'Comment <WORD> and I'll send you <asset>'
qc_checks: hook clip says the exact scripted words (Whisper)
cloned hook voice gender matches character (pitch check)
video duration == audio duration (CTA card absorbs remainder)
captions never collide with b-roll text or badge
compliance_notes: User videos provided as 'templates' are STRUCTURE-ONLY references: never composite their pixels, never reuse their frames as image-gen references, never clone their script wording (feedback 2026-06-10)
No spoken digital-avatar disclosure per owner decision; set the platform synthetic-media flag at upload instead
Phone/UI shots must use real product pixels (no generated readable UI)
